name: Huangtian Dadi

visual_attributes.morphology: Anthropomorphic figure with regal and divine features
distinctive_features: Crown prince attire from the kingdom of Pure Felicity and Majestic Heavenly Lights and Ornaments
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Seated on a celestial throne, surrounded by heavenly ornaments

domain_specific_visual.CELESTIAL_CROWN: Wears a crown symbolizing supreme authority

functional_attributes.primary_function: Supreme sovereign of all deities in heaven

domain_specific_functional.CREATOR_OF_HUMANS: Fashioned the first humans from clay

relationships.description: Father of Zhinü, the Weaver Girl
Assistant to one of the Three Pure Ones in Taoist theology

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents supreme authority and benevolence in Chinese culture

contrastive_constraints: NOT a generic king or emperor
NOT solely a Taoist figure without Buddhist connections
